page: Amazon Web Services c5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the c5.4xlarge in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The processor achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1494.39 and a multi-core score of 15741.25, supported by hardware-accelerated instructions including AVX and AVX-512F. Memory performance tests show a read-only cached bandwidth of 877.39 GB/sec, which drops to 79.46 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and a write bandwidth of 47.95 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the server demonstrated a static web serving throughput of 12.86 GB/sec and processed 15.23 million Redis SET operations per second.

Due to the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs, this instance relies entirely on network-attached storage and is unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning training. While it handles small-scale language model inference reasonably well—achieving 147.35 tokens per second for text generation on a 135M parameter model—performance drops significantly to 22.69 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model. The c5.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, non-storage-intensive workloads such as software compilation, batch processing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.4 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average tier performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15741.25 and a static web serving throughput of 12.86 GB/sec. Supported by AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets, it is well-suited for compute-intensive tasks like software compilation, batch processing, and web serving, though its lack of local storage and GPU hardware limits its utility for large-scale database hosting or deep learning workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 16-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 5 Gbps network baseline, designed for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and balanced DDR4 memory for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs at 3.4 GHz.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz.
Features AVX and AVX-512F instruction set support for vector calculations.
Delivers 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU.
Optimized for software compilation, batch processing, and high-throughput web serving.
